'''Brendan Hands''' (born 12 February 1999) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as {{rlp|hk}} for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[Super League]].

==Playing career==
===Early career===
Hands was a [[Brothers Penrith]] junior who played both rugby league and basketball.<ref>{{cite web |last1=Jones |first1=Matt |title=Rugby league and basketball in good Hands |url=https://www.hawkesburygazette.com.au/story/274154/rugby-league-and-basketball-in-good-hands/ |website=Hawkesbury Gazette |access-date=6 April 2023 |date=1 August 2012}}</ref> From a young age his coaches said that he would be a NRL player.<ref name="Great kid with a ripper backstory. In under-6s, playing for Emu Plains, he crossed for 56 tries and his coach at the time said he’d one day be an NRL star. Was also a gun basketballer growing up.">{{cite news |last1=Crawley |first1=Paul |last2=Pollard |first2=Kyle |title=NRL 2021: The best rookies set to debut next season |work=[[The Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|The Daily Telegraph]] |publisher=Nationwide News Pty Ltd |date=23 December 2020 |location=Sydney, New South Wales}}</ref>

He would join the [[Penrith Panthers]] playing for their [[Jersey Flegg Cup]] under 20's team in 2019, winning the club's Junior Education Award.<ref>{{cite web |title=2019 Junior Education Award: Brendan Hands |url=https://www.penrithpanthers.com.au/news/2019/09/16/2019-junior-education-award-brendan-hands/ |website=penrithpanthers.com.au |access-date=6 April 2023 |date=16 September 2019}}</ref> In 2020, he was a part of Penrith's first grade squad.<ref name="Code"/>

===Parramatta Eels===
In 2022, Hands signed with the [[Parramatta Eels]] to play in the [[NSW Cup]] competition. Hands played 23 games for Parramatta's NSW Cup team in 2022.<ref name="Code"/>

In round 4 of the [[2023 NRL season]], Hands made his [[NRL]] debut for Parramatta<ref>{{cite web |title=Emotions high as Hands receives his NRL debut jersey |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2023/03/23/emotions-high-as-hands-receives-his-nrl-debut-jersey/ |website=NRL.com |access-date=6 April 2023 |date=23 March 2023}}</ref> against his former club, Penrith at [[Western Sydney Stadium]] and scored a try in his side's 17−16 golden point extra-time victory.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2023/03/23/moses-the-saviour-as-eels-win-dramatic-grand-final-rematch/|title=Moses the saviour as Eels win dramatic Grand Final rematch|website=www.nrl.com|date=23 March 2023 }}</ref><ref name="Code">{{cite web|url=https://www.codesports.com.au/nrl/eels/brendan-hands-has-dream-nrl-start-for-eels-against-the-club-he-grew-up-supporting/news-story/176744d32d8ae6c39c1c0bc54ced0e90/|last1=Whaley |first1=Pamela |title=Brendan Hands has dream NRL start for Eels against the club he grew up supporting|website=www.codesports.com.au|date=24 March 2023}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.news.com.au/sport/nrl/what-a-game-fans-lose-it-over-golden-point-nrl-madness/news-story/77ed9963f95c5508b370d1154bf8bf10/|title='What a game': Fans lose it over golden point NRL madness|website=www.news.com.au}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |last1=Bruce |first1=Jasper |title=NRL's new $6 million Mitchell Moses the hero as Parramatta Eels sink Penrith Panthers despite Nathan Cleary genius |url=https://www.penrithpanthers.com.au/news/2019/09/16/2019-junior-education-award-brendan-hands/ |website=7news.com.au |publisher=Seven West Media |access-date=6 April 2023 |date=24 March 2023}}</ref>

Hands played a total of 19 matches for Parramatta in the 2023 NRL season as the club finished 10th and missed the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.sportingnews.com/au/rugby-league/news/nrl-2023-parramatta-eels-season-review/87bc005ef8afccb71c8cd224#:~:text=Following%20a%201%2D4%20start,to%20the%20Eels'%20poor%20season./|title=NRL 2023: Parramatta Eels season review|website=www.sportingnews.com}}</ref>
After Parramatta's upset loss to the [[Wests Tigers]] in round 4 of the [[2024 NRL season]], Hands was demoted to reserve grade by Parramatta head coach [[Brad Arthur]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2024/04/02/nrl-team-lists-round-5/|title=NRL Round 5 Team Lists 2024|website=www.nrl.com}}</ref>

Hands played 19 games for Parramatta in the 2024 NRL season as the club finished 15th on the table. On 27 November 2024, Hands signed a two-year contract extension to remain at Parramatta until the end of 2026.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nine.com.au/sport/nrl/news-2024-the-mole-exclusive-parramatta-eels-season-review-reagan-campbell-gillard-future-20240907-p5k8qj.html/|title=The Mole's end of season review: What went wrong at the Parramatta Eels?|website=www.nine.com.au}}</ref>

===Toulouse Olympique (loan)===
On 30 July 2025, it was announced that Hands had signed with [[RFL Championship]] side [[Toulouse Olympique]] for the remainder of the 2025 season on loan. On 5 October 2025, Hands played in Toulouse Olympique's RFL Championship grand final victory over [[York RLFC|York]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2025/07/30/brendan-hands-departs-parramatta-eels/|title=Brendan Hands Departs Parramatta Eels|website=www.parraeels.com.au}}</ref>

On 22 October 2025, the Eels announced that Hands' loan deal with Toulouse would become permanent after Toulouse was promoted into the Super League.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2025-10-22 |title=Brendan Hands to Remain at Toulouse Olympique |url=https://www.parraeels.com.au/news/2025/10/22/brendan-hands-to-remain-at-toulouse-olympique/ |access-date=2025-10-22 |website=Parramatta Eels |language=en}}</ref>
